title: ser vs estar (to be)
explanationMd: Portuguese has two verbs for 'to be'. **ser** is for permanent/defining traits (identity, origin, time). **estar** is for temporary states (location, mood, current condition).

title: Present tense — regular -ar verbs
explanationMd: Regular **-ar** verbs (like *falar*) take the endings -o, -a, -amos, -am in the present.

examples.text: Eu falo, você fala, nós falamos, eles falam.
examples.translation: I speak, you speak, we speak, they speak.

title: você vs tu (you)
explanationMd: Most of Brazil uses **você** for 'you' (with third-person verb forms). Some regions (e.g. the South, parts of the Northeast) use **tu**. *O senhor / a senhora* is the formal, respectful address.

examples.translation: Do you speak English? (neutral, standard BR)
